channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Telangana Council Extends LAWCET Counselling Dates
item.description: The Telangana Council of Higher Education (TGCHE) has announced an extension of the LAWCET 2026 counselling period, allowing eligible candidates to complete their online registration and verification ...

item.title: Prince Harry Engages with Invictus Games Participants Amid Personal and Public Challenges
item.description: Prince Harry participated in the Invictus Games event at the National Exhibition Centre in Birmingham, where he engaged with injured military veterans and promoted the cause close to his heart. Despit...

item.title: Marilyn Monroe Recognized as a 1950s Icon in TIME Magazine's '100 Women of the Year' Profile
item.description: This article highlights Marilyn Monroe's selection by TIME Magazine as part of its '100 Women of the Year' project, celebrating her legacy as an iconic figure from the 1950s. While she didn't appear o...

item.title: Nissan Tekton set to enter South African market as local production ends
item.description: Nissan has ended its vehicle production in South Africa after over 60 years, with the new Tekton SUV set to become a key model in the local market. Developed in partnership with Renault in India, the ...

item.title: Beef Price-Fixing Settlement: Companies Agree to $8 Million Payment
item.description: A proposed $8-million settlement has been reached between several major meat producers and Canadian law firms over allegations of price-fixing in the beef industry. Companies including JBS USA, Swift ...

item.title: Spain Prepare for Crucial World Cup Quarterfinal Against Belgium
item.description: Spain's national football team has advanced to the quarterfinals of the FIFA World Cup 2026 without conceding a single goal, setting up a high-stakes encounter against Belgium. Coach Luis de la Fuente...

item.title: Albanese's Comments on Kylie Minogue and Labor's Policy on Gambling and Housing
item.description: The article discusses Prime Minister Anthony Albanese's controversial remarks about his attraction to Kylie Minogue during a podcast interview, which sparked significant media backlash. While critics ...

item.title: DNA Testing as Routine Post-Birth Care in Nigeria to Address Paternity Disputes and Hospital Errors
item.description: The article discusses how DNA testing is increasingly becoming essential in Nigeria's post-birth care system. It highlights statistics showing that about 27% of paternity tests conducted by SURJEN Hea...

item.title: Defense Secretary Hegseth Overrides Suspension of Apache Pilots for Low-Altitude Beach Flights
item.description: U.S. Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth has reinstated eight Apache helicopter pilots suspended for flying at low altitudes over crowded South Carolina beaches during the Fourth of July celebrations. T...

item.title: Tanzania boosts energy investment efforts to drive economic growth
item.description: Tanzania is enhancing its efforts to attract energy investments to fuel economic growth and development. At the Energy Forum during the 50th Dar es Salaam International Trade Fair, Petroleum Upstream ...

item.title: Anti-Corruption Rally Draws Thousands in Manila
item.description: A significant anti-corruption rally was held in Manila, drawing thousands of participants who demanded accountability and transparency from government officials. The event, organized by the United Peo...

item.title: Nigel Farage's Byelection and the Political Controversy in Clacton
item.description: The article discusses the Clacton byelection, focusing on Nigel Farage's campaign against the 'establishment' and the eclectic mix of candidates, including Laurence Fox and Piers Corbyn. It critiques ...

item.title: SAHRC inquiry concludes with demands for retailer accountability in food crisis
item.description: The South African Human Rights Commission (SAHRC) concluded a five-day inquiry into the country's food system, highlighting the need for major retailers to address their role in rising food prices. Th...

item.title: Mwanza Region's Educational and Sports Achievements Highlighted
item.description: Mwanza Region in Tanzania has solidified its position as a national leader in education and sports after securing its fifth consecutive victory in the Inter-Secondary School tournament. Regional Commi...

item.title: Patricia Greene, longtime voice of Jill Archer in BBC Radio 4's The Archers, dies aged 94
item.description: Patricia Greene, affectionately known as Paddy Greene, has died at the age of 94 after a remarkable career spanning almost seven decades as Jill Archer in BBC Radio 4's The Archers. Joining the progra...

item.title: Scientists Solve Dendrite-Induced Short Circuits in Solid-State Batteries
item.description: Researchers at the Max Planck Institute for Sustainable Materials have identified how soft lithium dendrites penetrate hard ceramic electrolytes in solid-state batteries, causing short circuits. This ...

item.title: Nova Scotia seeks proposals to determine future of Halifax's Mackay Bridge
item.description: Nova Scotia has issued a request for proposals (RFP) to explore the potential future of Halifax’s Mackay Bridge, a key infrastructure project in the province. The initiative aims to assess options for...

item.title: Caleb Amisi Warns Irungu Kang'ata Against Taking Over Linda Mwananchi Movement
item.description: Saboti MP Caleb Amisi has publicly criticized Murang'a Governor Irungu Kang'ata for attempting to take control of the Linda Mwananchi political movement. Amisi accused Kang'ata of having ulterior moti...

item.title: A Man of the People: A Critical Analysis of Corruption and Leadership in Modern Africa
item.description: The article reviews Chinua Achebe’s novel 'A Man of the People,' highlighting its enduring relevance to contemporary African political issues. It examines how corruption, state capture, and unethical ...

item.title: Philippine Military Expands Security Measures to Protect Bajo de Masinloc Amid Tensions
item.description: Defense Secretary Gilberto Teodoro Jr. announced that the military is enhancing security measures to protect Panatag (Scarborough) Shoal and expand patrols across the Philippines' maritime territory. ...
